reasoning: Based on historical climate data for Saint-Girons, France, late October is significantly warmer than January. The expected average daily temperature around October 26th is roughly 12.5°C, while late January (such as January 24th) averages around 6°C. Assuming typical daily temperature variability (standard deviation around 4°C for both months), the difference between a single random January day and a random late October day has a mean of about -6.5°C and a standard deviation of 5.65°C. Thus, the probability of the January day being warmer is statistically roughly 10% to 15%. Given the absence of specific listed dates in the text, it is interpreted that the query targets the closing date (late January), making the likelihood of it being warmer than October 26th quite low.
